Medica Europe is hiring a Regulatory Affairs Specialist for a permanent position in Lyon with hybrid working. The role supports the European medical-device portfolio and works closely with Quality, Clinical, Product Development and Manufacturing. Candidates should have at least two years of experience in medical-device regulatory affairs or quality assurance.

Responsibilities include preparing and maintaining EU MDR technical documentation, assessing product and manufacturing changes, coordinating evidence for regulatory submissions, answering notified-body questions and keeping submission plans current. The specialist will also contribute to clinical-evaluation updates, post-market surveillance, vigilance activities, risk-management documentation and continuous improvement of regulatory processes.

The successful candidate understands EU MDR and ISO 13485, writes clear technical documents in English, manages several priorities and can coordinate subject-matter experts without losing traceability. Experience with Class II devices, CAPA, document control and notified-body interactions is valued. The position requires professional English and confident cross-functional communication. No software-development or data-science experience is required.
